title: calandrinia pygmaea
intro: Calandrinia pygmaea, also known as the dwarf rock purslane, is a small succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Calandrinia pygmaea are typically made from the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as a tea or used in salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
